structured_fields.name: first_publish_year
structured_fields.description: Year the book was first published

structured_fields.name: genres
structured_fields.description: Broad literary or non-fiction genres this book belongs to

structured_fields.name: themes
structured_fields.description: Core thematic ideas explored in the book — short phrases, not sentences

structured_fields.name: key_points
structured_fields.description: Concise bullet-style insights capturing the most important ideas or arguments in the book. Each as a standalone sentence.
example: Small 1% improvements compound into remarkable results over time
Habits are driven by cue, craving, response and reward
Identity-based habits are more durable than outcome-based ones

structured_fields.name: related_books
structured_fields.description: Real books with similar themes or complementary ideas. Format: 'Title by Author'
example: The Power of Habit by Charles Duhigg
Thinking, Fast and Slow by Daniel Kahneman

freetext_fields.name: summary
freetext_fields.description: A well-crafted summary of the book — its central argument, narrative arc, and what makes it significant. Use multiple concise paragraphs with blank lines between them. Respect the requested depth and style.

freetext_fields.name: why_it_matters
freetext_fields.description: A short paragraph on why this book is relevant today — its cultural, intellectual or practical significance

prompt_hints.tone: literary and insightful — written for an intellectually curious reader
avoid: plot spoilers for fiction without warning
inventing quotes or attributing false claims to the author
generic summaries that could apply to any book in the genre
prefer: capturing the author's specific voice and argument
themes as short evocative phrases not full sentences
related books that genuinely complement this one
summary tone that subtly reflects the requested style
